unit.name: GAN minimax objective and instability
unit.description: Derives the GAN minimax game and explains why its training signal is hard to diagnose.
unit.summary: The GAN objective is formalized as a minimax game between G and D [ts=553s]. With G fixed, D is trained as a binary classifier: maximize log D(x) on real data and log(1 - D(G(z))) on generated data so real samples get D(x)=1 and fake samples get D(x)=0 [ts=596s], [ts=664s]. With D fixed, the real-data term no longer depends on G, so G minimizes the fake-data term to make D classify generated samples as real [ts=720s]. Training alternates gradient ascent on D and gradient descent on G over the value V(G,D) [ts=784s], [ts=818s]. A crucial diagnostic warning is that V is not a conventional loss: its absolute value does not directly indicate sample quality or convergence because it depends on both D and G [ts=847s]. This makes GANs effective but difficult to train, tune, and diagnose, since the problem is inherently unstable and lacks a reliable decreasing loss curve [ts=917s].

key_points.text: GANs jointly train G and D with a minimax game.
For fixed G, D maximizes log D(x) on real data and log(1-D(G(z))) on generated data.
For fixed D, G cares only about the fake-sample term and tries to make D classify fake samples as real.
Training alternates gradient ascent for D and gradient descent for G on V(G,D).
The minimax value V is not an overall loss, so its absolute value is not a reliable training-quality metric.
GAN objectives are unstable because the same quantity is maximized and minimized by different networks.

kp_catalog.name: GAN minimax objective
kp_catalog.description: The objective min_G max_D E_x log D(x)+E_z log(1-D(G(z))) defines the adversarial game.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because gAN minimax objective formalizes how the objective min_G max_D E_x log D(x)+E_z log(1-D(G(z))) defines the adversarial game, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Alternating adversarial optimization
kp_catalog.description: GAN training alternates gradient ascent on D and gradient descent on G over V(G,D).
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because alternating adversarial optimization clarifies gAN training alternates gradient ascent on D and gradient descent on G over V(G,D), sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: GAN instability diagnostics
kp_catalog.description: GAN value and loss curves are not reliable progress indicators because the game is non-stationary and jointly adversarial.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because gAN instability diagnostics clarifies gAN value and loss curves are not reliable progress indicators because the game is non-stationary and jointly adversarial, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
